formulas.name: FIFO Property
formulas.formula: The first element added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.
formulas.notes: This is a fundamental property of a Queue data structure.

formulas.name: Enqueue Operation
formulas.formula: Add an element to the end of the queue.
formulas.notes: This is the operation used to add new elements to the queue.
formulas.keyPoints: New elements are added at the end of the queue.

formulas.name: Dequeue Operation
formulas.formula: Remove an element from the front of the queue.
formulas.notes: This is the operation used to remove elements from the queue.
formulas.keyPoints: Elements are removed from the front of the queue.

formulas.name: Queue Operations
formulas.notes: These are the basic operations that can be performed on a Queue.
formulas.keyPoints: These operations are used to manipulate the elements in the queue.

formulas.name: Queue Representation
formulas.notes: A Queue can be represented using an array or a linked list.
formulas.keyPoints: Arrays and linked lists are two common ways to implement a Queue.

formulas.name: Queue Properties
formulas.notes: A Queue has several properties that define its behavior.
formulas.keyPoints: These properties are essential to understand the behavior of a Queue.
